summary: Justin Brennan coaches mentees on setting up professional infrastructure (website, CRM, LLC, pitch deck, landing pages) to attract capital before securing deals. He demonstrates how establishing credibility through online presence and team positioning enables easier fundraising when deals arrive, using his 27-unit deal ($900K raised) as proof of concept.
takeaways: Set up LLC, website, landing page, and pitch deck 1-3 months BEFORE getting a deal—look the part before you have the part
Trust is the foundation: investors ask 'Do I trust you'll return my $25K, $50K?' before evaluating the deal itself
Build a CRM database of 25+ soft-committed contacts; from 25 contacts, expect ~11 responses and ~7 actual investors (27-unit deal: 7 of 11 invested ~$400K total)
Minimum investor commitment threshold: ~$25K per investor; position returns at 15-20% annually to attract capital
Use Loom (free screen-recording tool) to create summary videos by walking through pitch deck—share via email, text, YouTube without mass marketing compliance issues
Website should include: About Us with your photo, Investment Strategy, Opportunities pages, Contact form linking to Calendly, downloadable pitch deck behind form submission
Connect all tools pre-deal: website → CRM contacts → pitch deck → summary video → Calendly scheduling = seamless investor flow when deal launches

skill_candidates.change: Build pre-deal infrastructure (website, CRM, LLC, pitch deck, landing pages) 1-3 months before sourcing to enable faster capital attraction
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin's 27-unit deal ($900K raised, zero prior multifamily track record) closed because he had professional online presence, LLC, pitch deck, and landing page pre-positioned before broker/lender engagement; mentees like Michael and Gideon shown live examples of deal landing pages funneling investors through contact forms to Calendly scheduling

skill_candidates.change: Create soft-commit contact database of 25+ before deal closes; expect 44% response rate (11 of 25) and 64% conversion of responders (7 of 11) to actual investors
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin's 27-unit deal sourced 7 investors from initial 25-person outreach; framework: send pitch deck + summary video to CRM list → capture investor info → legal structure (LLC for small deals, PPM for syndication) to close capital

skill_candidates.change: Systematize investor pipeline: use free tools (Loom video, Calendly, Go High Level CRM) instead of ad-hoc outreach to scale deal marketing phase
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin demonstrated landing page workflow: investor clicks → fills contact form → downloads pitch deck → books call on Calendly → auto-added to CRM; eliminates manual follow-up chaos during deal closing

title: Weekly Coaching_10-23-25 | Justin Brennan
